# Andrew Carnegie Book List

Carnegie Library of Pittsburgh

Andrew Carnegie was a competitive, often ruthless businessman . He was also a philanthropist who gave away more than $350 million during his lifetime. During our anniversary year, CLP is celebrating the institution that has grown and evolved out of Carnegie’s original gift of a library building , museum and music hall . If you’d like to learn more about the man behind that gift, here are some titles we recommend that show the good – and the bad – about the man whose name our libraries bear.

You can sign up for a [free library card here](https://librarycatalog.einetwork.net/MyResearch/GetCard) . If you are new to our [eResources](https://www.carnegielibrary.org/eresources/) , check out these [tutorial videos](https://www.carnegielibrary.org/eresource-tutorial-videos/) on how to get started.

If you have any additional questions, you can contact a librarian through [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/CarnegieLibraryOfPittsburgh/) , [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/carnegielibrary/?hl=en) or [Twitter](https://twitter.com/carnegielibrary) . You can also call us at 412-622-3114 or email us at [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) .

[](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C2693130)

### [Meet You in Hell: Andrew Carnegie, Henry Clay Frick, and the Bitter Partnership that Transformed America](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C2693130)

by Les Standiford

The 1892 Homestead Steel Strike was a turning point in labor history, and the relationship between business partners Carnegie and Frick. This book talks about the strike and the events that led to the two men becoming bitter enemies. While Carnegie’s business practices might not pass muster compared to modern ones , the strike may be the event that tarnished his legacy the most. You can also check out this title as an eBook or audio book from [Overdrive/Libby](https://acla.overdrive.com/search?query=meet+you+in+hell) .

* * *

[](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C1375999)

### [The Battle for Homestead, 1880-1892: Politics, Culture, and Steel](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C1375999)

by Paul Krause

For more information about the strike, we recommend this book which looks at the workers’ experiences in the steel mills, the events that led to the lockout, and the fallout afterward.

* * *

[](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C2665823)

### [Andrew Carnegie](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C2665823)

by David Nasaw

Clocking in at 878 pages, this book attempts to address the seemingly contradictory – calculating businessman and philanthropist — aspects of Andrew Carnegie’s personality. The book begins wi th Carnegie’s youth in a struggling family of Scottish weavers and describes his rise to wealth and fame after emigrating to Pittsburgh. You can also check this title out as eAudio on [Hoopla](https://www.hoopladigital.com/title/12161763) and as an eBook or audio book from [Overdrive](https://acla.overdrive.com/search?query=andrew+carnegie+nasaw) /Libby.

* * *

[](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C1296222)

### [Andrew Carnegie](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C1296222)

by Joseph Frazier Wall

Considered by many to be the definitive Carnegie biography, this detailed book explores Carnegie’s business dealings, the philosophies he applied to his world, and his personal life. Fair warning, this one is another whopper at 1047 pages of text.

* * *

[](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C3163334)

### [The Autobiography of Andrew Carnegie](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C3163334)

by Andrew Carnegie

This is Carnegie in his own words, as he wanted to be remembered. He was a prolific writer, penning many letters and essays about his thoughts on the world, but this is his version of his life and the decisions he made.

You can also check this out as an eBook from [OverDrive](https://www.overdrive.com/media/784399/autobiography-of-andrew-carnegie) /Libby or eAudio on [Hoopla](https://www.hoopladigital.com/title/10756097) .

* * *

[](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C2558038)

### [The Tycoons: How Andrew Carnegie, John D. Rockerfeller, Jay Gould, and J.P. Morgan Invented the American Supereconomy](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C2558038)

by Charles R. Morris

This book places Carnegie in context with the other big-business tycoons of his day . These men moved in the same circles and competed with each other – socially and in business – and this book focuses on the economic impact of their companies and decisions. You can also check this out as an audio book from [Overdrive/Libby](https://acla.overdrive.com/media/2140220) .

* * *

[](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C3859927)

### [Andrew Carnegie: Rags to Riches, Power to Peace](https://acl.bibliocommons.com/v2/record/S209C3859927)

by Brian Cox (Narrator)

This film documents Carnegie’s life and business accomplishments, and includes interviews with many of the top experts who have written about him.

* * *
